title: carex conferta var. lycurus
intro: Carex conferta var. lycurus, also known as coastal sedge or Lycurus grass, is a type of perennial grass native to the coastal regions of New Zealand. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating wounds and infections. The health benefits of Carex conferta var. lycurus include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and poultices to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
